gaps: No test case covers the Device Settings screen structure or section visibility for any device type, let alone Voyager Focus 2.
No test case validates that the 'Apps' section (or any specific section) appears in Device Settings after pairing a specific device model.
No test case covers device-specific settings screen differences — e.g., Voyager Focus 2 may have different available sections compared to Voyager Free 60+ or other devices.
No test case validates the post-pairing Device Settings UI for Voyager Focus 2 on iOS specifically.
No lifecycle or state-transition tests exist for Device Settings sections (e.g., what happens to section visibility after reconnect, app restart, or firmware update).

generated_testcases.title: TC-001: Verify Apps section appears in Device Settings after pairing Voyager Focus 2 on iOS
steps: Ensure Voyager Focus 2 is in pairing mode (power off, then hold the Call button until LED flashes red/blue).
Open Poly Studio Mobile app on iOS.
If no device is paired, tap 'Add Device' and follow pairing flow to pair Voyager Focus 2.
Once paired and connected, tap the Voyager Focus 2 device name tile on the home screen.
Observe the Device Settings screen that appears.
Scroll through all available sections in Device Settings.
Verify the presence of the 'Apps' section in the settings list.
generated_testcases.expected: The 'Apps' section is visible in the Device Settings screen for Voyager Focus 2 on iOS. All expected sections (e.g., Audio Settings, Device Info, Apps, etc.) are displayed correctly.

generated_testcases.title: TC-002: Verify Apps section persists in Device Settings after app restart on Voyager Focus 2 (iOS)
steps: Pair and connect Voyager Focus 2 to the iOS device following standard pairing flow.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap Voyager Focus 2 → Device Settings.
Confirm the 'Apps' section is visible.
Press the Home button to background the app.
Swipe up on the app preview to fully kill the Poly Studio Mobile app process.
Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the Voyager Focus 2 device name tile.
Observe the Device Settings screen and scroll to find the 'Apps' section.
generated_testcases.expected: The 'Apps' section remains visible in Device Settings after app force-kill and relaunch. No sections are missing or altered.

generated_testcases.title: TC-003: Verify Apps section visibility after Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect on Voyager Focus 2 (iOS)
steps: Pair and connect Voyager Focus 2 to the iOS device.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap Voyager Focus 2 → Device Settings.
Confirm the 'Apps' section is visible.
On the iOS device, go to Settings → Bluetooth → tap the 'i' icon next to Voyager Focus 2 → tap 'Disconnect'.
Wait 5 seconds.
Reconnect Voyager Focus 2 via Bluetooth settings or by power-cycling the headset.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap Voyager Focus 2 device name.
Observe the Device Settings screen and check for the 'Apps' section.
generated_testcases.expected: The 'Apps' section is visible in Device Settings after Bluetooth disconnect and reconnection. The Device Settings screen structure is fully restored.

generated_testcases.title: TC-004: Verify Device Settings section list for Voyager Focus 2 vs Voyager Free 60+ (iOS) — device-specific differences
steps: Pair and connect Voyager Focus 2 to the iOS device.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap Voyager Focus 2 → Device Settings.
Note all visible sections (e.g., Audio Settings, Device Info, Apps, etc.).
Disconnect Voyager Focus 2.
Pair and connect Voyager Free 60+ to the same iOS device.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap Voyager Free 60+ → Device Settings.
Note all visible sections for Voyager Free 60+.
Compare the two section lists side by side.
generated_testcases.expected: The Device Settings screen shows device-appropriate sections. Voyager Focus 2 includes the 'Apps' section. Voyager Free 60+ may have different sections (e.g., Touchscreen Charge Case settings). The section list is correct for each device model.

generated_testcases.title: TC-005: Verify Apps section visibility after firmware update on Voyager Focus 2 (iOS)
steps: Pair and connect Voyager Focus 2 to the iOS device.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap Voyager Focus 2 → Device Settings.
Confirm the 'Apps' section is visible.
Navigate to Device Info → Firmware Update.
If an update is available, proceed with the firmware update to completion.
After update completes and device reconnects, tap Voyager Focus 2 → Device Settings.
Scroll through all sections and verify the 'Apps' section is present.
generated_testcases.expected: The 'Apps' section remains visible in Device Settings after a firmware update completes. No sections are lost or altered due to the update process.

generated_testcases.title: TC-006: Verify Apps section visibility after phone reboot on Voyager Focus 2 (iOS)
steps: Pair and connect Voyager Focus 2 to the iOS device.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap Voyager Focus 2 → Device Settings.
Confirm the 'Apps' section is visible.
Close the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Reboot the iOS device.
After reboot, ensure Bluetooth is enabled on the iOS device.
Wait for Voyager Focus 2 to auto-reconnect (or manually reconnect via Bluetooth settings).
Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap Voyager Focus 2 device name.
Observe the Device Settings screen and verify the 'Apps' section.
generated_testcases.expected: The 'Apps' section is visible in Device Settings after a full phone reboot and reconnection. The Device Settings screen structure is fully restored.

generated_testcases.title: TC-007: Verify Apps section visibility after unpairing and re-pairing Voyager Focus 2 (iOS)
steps: Pair and connect Voyager Focus 2 to the iOS device.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap Voyager Focus 2 → Device Settings.
Confirm the 'Apps' section is visible.
On iOS, go to Settings → Bluetooth → tap the 'i' icon next to Voyager Focus 2 → tap 'Forget This Device'.
Confirm the unpairing action.
Put Voyager Focus 2 back into pairing mode (hold Call button until LED flashes red/blue).
Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap 'Add Device' and re-pair Voyager Focus 2.
After successful pairing, tap the Voyager Focus 2 device name tile.
Observe the Device Settings screen and check for the 'Apps' section.
generated_testcases.expected: The 'Apps' section appears in Device Settings after re-pairing Voyager Focus 2. The full Device Settings screen structure is correctly populated for a fresh pairing.

generated_testcases.title: TC-008: Verify all expected sections in Device Settings for Voyager Focus 2 on iOS
steps: Pair and connect Voyager Focus 2 to the iOS device.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app → tap Voyager Focus 2 → Device Settings.
Scroll through the entire Device Settings screen from top to bottom.
Document every section heading visible (e.g., Audio Settings, Device Info, Apps, etc.).
Verify that no expected sections are missing and no unexpected/duplicate sections appear.
generated_testcases.expected: The Device Settings screen for Voyager Focus 2 displays all expected sections correctly. The 'Apps' section is present along with other standard sections (Audio Settings, Device Info, etc.). No sections are missing or duplicated.
